analysis_result: 31 samples are sporadic (NF1 Status = 'Sporadic') in the MOESM4 Clinical Data table.
denominator_used: N = 108; definition: all rows in the MOESM4 'Clinical Data' sheet with non-null Tumor_ID (per data context note).
assumptions_made: Data context note was followed: filtered Tumor_ID non-null; used the first row as the header; exact string match on 'Sporadic' in the 'NF1 Status' column (case-sensitive); no missing values in the NF1 Status column for the filtered 108 rows.
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
  - File: 41467_2023_38432_MOESM4_ESM.xlsx
  - Sheet: 'Clinical Data' (only sheet)
  - Shape after reading with header=0: 108 rows x 15 columns
  - Columns: Tumor_ID, Tumor Type, Age, Gender, NF1 Status, Location, Recurrence, Time to Recurrence or Last Follow Up, Methylation_Group, Methylation, Sentrix_ID, RNA-Seq, RNA label, WES, WES label

B. COHORT DEFINITION
  - Start with all rows in the 'Clinical Data' sheet: 108
  - Filter Tumor_ID non-null (per data context note): 108 remaining
  - Final analytic cohort: 108 samples

C. VARIABLE CODING
  - Column: 'NF1 Status'
  - Value counts:
      NF1: 77
      Sporadic: 31
  - No missing values.

D. COMPUTATION
  - Count of rows where NF1 Status == 'Sporadic'
  - Numerator: (df['NF1 Status'] == 'Sporadic').sum() = 31
  - Denominator: len(df) = 108

E. RESULT
  - Number of Sporadic samples: 31 (28.7% of 108)

F. CAVEATS
  - None material. Exact string match used. NF1 Status column had only two values ('NF1' and 'Sporadic') with no NaN.
